evals.name: fan-out-sequential-gets
evals.prompt:
this concourse job is taking ~9 minutes and most of that is the first stage. heres the relevant slice from our pipeline.yml:

```yaml
jobs:
  - name: build-and-publish
    plan:
      - get: app-src
        trigger: true
      - get: ci
      - get: golang-builder
      - get: deploy-keys
      - get: chart-repo
      - get: version
      - task: unit
        file: ci/tasks/unit/task.yml
      - task: build
        file: ci/tasks/build/task.yml
      - put: app-image
        params: { image: build/image.tar }
```

basic gut says theres no reason all those gets need to run sequentially but i don't want to break the trigger behavior or end up paying for 30 cold workers. how do we speed this up? assume the worker pool is healthy and we have 4-6 concourse workers normally available.
evals.expected_output: Rewritten plan that wraps the first 6 gets in `in_parallel` with appropriate `fail_fast: true` and `limit:`. Trigger behavior preserved (still on `app-src`). Brief explanation of why this is safe (gets are independent) and what `fail_fast`/`limit` do.

assertions.name: wraps gets in in_parallel
assertions.description: Output YAML wraps the 6 gets in an `in_parallel:` block

assertions.name: preserves trigger on app-src
assertions.description: `trigger: true` is preserved on the `app-src` get

assertions.name: recommends fail_fast
assertions.description: Output includes `fail_fast: true` on the in_parallel block, with a brief reason

assertions.name: recommends limit
assertions.description: Output includes a `limit:` (4-6 is reasonable given 4-6 workers) so worker pool isn't blown out

assertions.name: task and put remain sequential after gets
assertions.description: The `task: unit`, `task: build`, and `put: app-image` steps stay sequential after the gets — they aren't moved into the in_parallel block

assertions.name: explains why it's safe
assertions.description: Response briefly explains why parallelizing gets is safe (gets are independent, no shared state, plan still waits for slowest get before running tasks)

evals.name: across-matrix-with-put
evals.prompt:
trying to build a matrix that runs our test task across 3 go versions (1.22, 1.23, 1.24) and 2 OSes (linux, darwin), then publishes the resulting test reports as an s3 artifact. concourse keeps barfing on this and i think `across` is the issue but i can't tell what's wrong. current attempt:

```yaml
jobs:
  - name: matrix-test
    plan:
      - across:
          - var: go_v
            values: ["1.22", "1.23", "1.24"]
          - var: os
            values: ["linux", "darwin"]
        do:
          - get: source
            trigger: true
          - task: test
            file: source/ci/test.yml
            vars:
              go: ((.:go_v))
              os: ((.:os))
          - put: test-reports
            params:
              file: source/reports/((.:os))-((.:go_v)).tgz
```

please show me a working version. i want all 6 combos to run in parallel if there are workers available, and i want the s3 publish to actually work.
evals.expected_output: Working pipeline. Gets pulled OUT of `across` (across does not support `get`/`put` directly). The `put` is also pulled out of `across` (or replaced with a single put that uploads a combined artifact). `max_in_flight: all` (or integer) and `fail_fast: true` set. Brief explanation of the across caveat: outputs don't escape, get/put not supported.

assertions.name: states across does not work with get/put
assertions.description: Response explicitly states (or quotes) that the `across` step does NOT work with `get` or `put` steps

assertions.name: moves get out of across
assertions.description: Final YAML places `get: source` BEFORE / OUTSIDE the `across` block, not inside

assertions.name: moves put out of across
assertions.description: Final YAML places `put: test-reports` OUTSIDE the `across` block — either as a single put after across, or replaces across entirely with `in_parallel`

assertions.name: mentions outputs do not escape across
assertions.description: Response notes that outputs from steps inside `across` are not available to steps outside (relevant to why per-combo puts don't work)

assertions.name: max_in_flight all
assertions.description: Final YAML includes `max_in_flight: all` (or an explicit integer) to run combos in parallel

assertions.name: preserves trigger semantics
assertions.description: Final pipeline still triggers on new versions of `source` (trigger: true preserved)

assertions.name: matrix coverage
assertions.description: All 6 combinations (3 go × 2 os) are still produced by the corrected pipeline

evals.name: fan-in-double-trigger
evals.prompt:
weird issue: every time we land a commit on main, our `deploy-staging` job runs **twice**, back to back, with the same SHA. it depends on two upstream jobs (`unit` and `integration`) that both pass. when both of them finish, deploy-staging fires twice. occasional CI flake means we sometimes get *three* runs.

relevant chunk:

```yaml
  - name: deploy-staging
    plan:
      - in_parallel:
          - get: app-image
            trigger: true
            passed: [unit]
          - get: source
            trigger: true
            passed: [integration]
      - task: deploy
        file: source/ci/deploy.yml
```

why is it double-running and how do we fix it without losing the "deploy on every successful main commit" behavior? we still want the deploy to be triggered automatically.
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis: two gets with `trigger: true` and different `passed:` lists means each get triggers when its respective upstream lands a new version, so the job runs once per upstream. Plus the `passed:` lists are split, so version skew is also possible. Fix: list both parents in `passed:` on each get, set `trigger: true` on only one (the canonical input), keep the other as a constraint-only get. Show corrected YAML.

assertions.name: diagnoses two triggers as cause
assertions.description: Response identifies that two `trigger: true` gets cause two independent triggers, hence two runs

assertions.name: fix uses single trigger
assertions.description: Final YAML has `trigger: true` on EXACTLY ONE get (not both)

assertions.name: fix mirrors passed lists
assertions.description: Final YAML lists BOTH `unit` AND `integration` in `passed:` on BOTH gets (locks to the same end-to-end version)

assertions.name: calls out version skew risk
assertions.description: Response notes that the original split `passed:` lists also risk fetching mismatched versions of `app-image` and `source`

assertions.name: preserves auto-deploy
assertions.description: Response confirms (or shows) that the fix still auto-deploys on every successful main commit — not converted to manual

assertions.name: valid yaml
assertions.description: Output YAML is syntactically valid Concourse v8 syntax (correct indentation, valid keys)
